stem:
8P + 30Y = 540

At a school carnival, students can win colored tokens that are worth a different number of points depending on the color. One student won P purple tokens and Y yellow tokens worth a total of 540 points. The given equation represents this situation. How many more points is a yellow token worth than a purple token?
rationale: The coefficients of P and Y in the equation are the per-token point values. A purple token is worth 8 points and a yellow token is worth 30 points, so a yellow token is worth 30 − 8 = 22 more points than a purple token.
